project: kcl/dateutils — port of FPC DateUtils to bash (kklass Pascal DSL static class)
status: COMPLETE — all phases P0-P7 done. 185 public methods; dateutils suite 123/123 on bash 5.2, functional on 5.3; full kcl+kklass sweep 1738/0; hot paths <=0.3ms/call, zero forks; tw16040 parity file complete (17/17). 3 plan inaccuracies corrected (TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md).
notes: Single source of truth for the dateutils implementation. Plan: kcl/dateutils/PLAN.md. Source: C:/projects/KKMindWave/VendorsCore/fpc/sources/main/packages/rtl-objpas/src/inc/dateutil.inc (3857 lines, 195 functions, ~160 public). Update task.status as work proceeds. The kcl+kklass baseline below must NEVER regress; every phase ends with a full sweep on bash 5.2 and a spot-check on 5.3.

baseline.bash_primary: GNU bash 5.2.37 (MSYS2) — thin static dispatch path (no funsub)
baseline.bash_secondary: GNU bash 5.3.9 (C:/bin/msys64) — funsub available; EPOCHREALTIME verified on both

suites_green.kcl_breakdown: tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155
suites_green._note: kcl_total corrected from an erroneous 1584 (never matched the per-suite breakdown); per-suite re-measured on bash 5.2 at the end of P0 (2026-07-11) and all green. tfile 120 & tpath 112 include the +4 constant-hardening pins from the prior session.

baseline.regression_rule: dateutils work must keep all of the above green; new suite adds on top (P0: +20 -> kcl_total 1409).

design_decisions.representation: KDT = integer milliseconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00, naive (no zone), proleptic Gregorian. Negative = before 1970. Bash 64-bit integer arithmetic only; no floats anywhere.
design_decisions.calendar_math: Hinnant days_from_civil / civil_from_days in pure bash integers; ISO-8601 week rules; zero `date` forks (EPOCHREALTIME for wall clock, printf %(%z)T for local offset).
design_decisions.class_shape: Pascal DSL static utility class `dateutils` (like tpath/tfile): static proc members, bodies as real bash functions, `build dateutils`. NO static vars — must stay on the thin capture-free dispatcher. Constants are top-level variables; bash has NO file scope, so they are process-wide globals — hence prefixed __KDT_* and declared readonly (behind a re-source guard) to prevent collisions/overwrites.
design_decisions.naming: FPC names in lowerCamelCase: YearOf -> dateutils.yearOf.
design_decisions.returns: results echoed; booleans echo true/false; multi-out echoes space-separated fields; Try* echo result + return status; Span* echo fixed-point 6dp strings; errors return 1 (stderr only under VERBOSE_KKLASS=debug).

goals: Faithful FPC DateUtils semantics (ISO weeks, approximate vs exact Between, month-end clamping, periodBetween borrow logic) on pure-bash integers.
Zero-fork hot paths; extraction/arithmetic <= ~0.3 ms/call via the thin static dispatcher.
Full ktest coverage with fixture tables for every boundary family (ISO week edges, leap centuries, pre-1970, month ends).

test_basis.seed_primary: C:/projects/KKMindWave/VendorsCore/fpc/sources/main/tests/webtbs/tw16040.pp (17 assertions) — ported 1:1 into tests/00X_FpcParity_*.sh, values verbatim, built up incrementally per phase, complete at P7 (scanDateTime).
seed_additional: tests/test/units/dateutil/tunitdt1.pp — far-future Unix roundtrips (15796372693 -> 2470-07-26 09:18:13; 2345-12-12 04:45:49 encode->unix->decode) -> P6

parity_assertions_by_phase.P6: JulianDateToDateTime(2455277.5)=2010-03-22 00:00; DateTimeToJulianDate roundtrip; MJD roundtrip; tunitdt1 far-future Unix fixtures

test_basis.new_coverage_protocol: Every test case NOT traceable to an FPC test file gets a row in kcl/dateutils/TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md: id (file+label), functions, concrete case, why FPC lacks it (boundary / bash-specific convention / representation-specific / cross-check), and the basis for the expected value (FPC source reading, independent calculation, ISO citation, GNU date output). That document is the review artifact for further analysis and potential upstreaming.

out_of_scope.reason: Mac OS classic timestamps — no bash use case.
16-bit DOS packed format — no bash use case.
KDT already IS the number; dateTimeDiff provided as signed ms.
FPC type-helper/class sugar over the same free functions.

P7.1 decision gate resolved: the practical subset (yyyy/mm/dd/hh/nn/ss/zzz + literals + elastic whitespace) covers the tw16040 assertion and real use; the full FPC matcher (month/day names, ampm, [] optionals, T/date-format tokens) is not needed.

phases.name: Scaffolding & core representation
phases.goal: Class skeleton + civil-calendar core + ISO parse/format + wall clock; the foundation every later phase builds on.
phases.result: dateutils.sh (class skeleton, __KDT_* readonly constants behind a re-source guard, Hinnant civil helpers, ISO fmt/parse, EPOCHREALTIME+%(%z)T wall clock, 9 constant getters); tests/001_Core.sh 20/20 on bash 5.2 and functional on 5.3 (thin dispatch confirmed on both; %(%z)T=+0300, EPOCHREALTIME present); tests/002_FpcParity.sh skeleton green; TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md seeded (19 P0 rows). Anchors verified: KDT 0=1970-01-01 Thu, KDT -1=1969-12-31 23:59:59.999, 1899-12-30=-2209161600000. Full kcl+kklass sweep green (no regression).

tasks.title: Directory, class skeleton, tests runner
tasks.fix: class dateutils ... end + build via kklass_pascal.sh; ktests runner mirroring other kcl suites; header comment documenting KDT and the no-static-var perf rule.
tasks.acceptance: source dateutils.sh succeeds on 5.2 and 5.3; dispatchers are thin (no capture); empty suite runs green.

tasks.title: Civil-calendar helpers (pure integer)
tasks.fix: Hinnant algorithms in bash $(( )); _split_kdt: KDT -> Y M D h m s ms with floor-division for negatives; _join_kdt inverse.
tasks.acceptance: Roundtrip grid: every (year in {1899,1900,1970,1999,2000,2004,2100,2400}) x (month 1..12) x (day 1,28,29,30,31 valid) x sample times; anchor: KDT 0 == 1970-01-01 Thu; negative KDT correct.

tasks.title: ISO 8601 format/parse helpers
tasks.fix: printf -v formatting; parse via bash regex: YYYY-MM-DD, hh:mm[:ss[.zzz]], date[T| ]time, optional Z/±hh[:]mm captured for P6.
tasks.acceptance: format(parse(s)) == normalize(s) for a fixture list incl. ms and T-separator forms; malformed inputs return 1 echoing nothing.

tasks.title: Wall clock + trivial constructors + public constant getters
tasks.note: Constant getters are static procs (class stays static-var-free -> thin dispatch); they echo the __KDT_* readonly globals. Rationale in PLAN.md ('Why not a TDateUtils class with static var constants').
tasks.fix: EPOCHREALTIME -> ms; local offset via printf '%(%z)T' cached per process (P0 verifies the builtin on MSYS2 5.2 and cygwin 5.3; fallback to a single cached `date +%z` fork only if broken).
tasks.acceptance: now within 2s of EPOCHREALTIME; nowUTC - now == -offset; today == dateOf(now); zero forks in the default path (verified by disabling PATH).

tasks.title: Test basis scaffolding: FPC parity seed file + TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md
tasks.fix: Create the FPC-parity test file seeded from tw16040.pp (assertions land per phase as their functions appear; values verbatim, never 'improved') and the TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md stub with the row format from PLAN.md. Every subsequently added non-FPC test case MUST add its row in the same change.
tasks.acceptance: Parity file skeleton runs green (0 checks initially is fine); TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md exists with format header; the per-phase acceptance blocks below reference their tw16040 assertions.

phases.name: Encode/Decode & validity
phases.result: 24 functions: encode/tryEncode/decode Date/Time/DateTime + encodeDateDay/decodeDateDay; isValid Date/Time/DateTime/DateDay/DateWeek/DateMonthWeek; isInLeapYear(date); daysInAMonth/daysInMonth/daysInAYear/daysInYear; weeksInAYear/weeksInYear. Internal helpers _is_leap/_days_in_month/_weeks_in_year/_valid_date/_valid_time/_debug. FPC quirks honored: IsValidTime accepts 24:00:00.000 (whole-day marker); IsInLeapYear takes a date; WeeksInAYear ISO rule verified (2004/2009/2015/2020/2026=53, 2005/2021=52). encode* return 1 on invalid (FPC raises). Tests: 003_EncodeDecode.sh (8), 004_Validity.sh (10), 002_FpcParity.sh P1 block (5 tw16040 anchors incl. IsInLeapYear=false). dateutils suite 43/43 on 5.2, functional on 5.3. Full sweep 1658/0 (kcl 1432 incl. dateutils 43, kklass 226) — no regression. TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md +18 rows.

tasks.title: encode*/decode* + try* variants
tasks.acceptance: encode(decode(x)) == x on the P0 grid; invalid inputs -> status 1 (try*) / status 1 + debug msg (encode*); decode echoes space-separated fields readable with read -r. FPC parity (tw16040): EncodeDateTime anchors 2010-03-22, 2010-03-23, 2011-03-26 19:15:30.555 and EncodeDate(2011,1,1) land in the parity file.

tasks.title: Validity + calendar size functions
tasks.acceptance: Leap fixtures: 1900 false, 2000 true, 2004 true, 2100 false; Feb lengths; weeksInAYear in {52,53} with ISO fixtures (2004->53, 2005->52, 2009->53). FPC parity (tw16040): IsInLeapYear(2011-03-26)=false.

phases.name: Extraction
phases.goal: All xxOf and xxOfTheYyy field extractors.
phases.result: 40 functions: yearOf..milliSecondOf, weekOf, dayOfTheWeek(ISO Mon=1..Sun=7)/dayOfTheMonth/dayOfTheYear, monthOfTheYear, weekOfTheYear/weekOfTheMonth (ISO-8601), isAM/isPM, the full hourOfThe*/minuteOfThe*/secondOfThe*/milliSecondOfThe* families, nthDayOfWeek, decodeDayOfWeekInMonth. Internal recursive ISO decoders _decode_date_week/_decode_date_month_week (faithful FPC DecodeDateWeek/DecodeDateMonthWeek ports, reused by P5). Tests: 005_Extraction.sh (7), 006_OfTheFamilies.sh (5), 002_FpcParity.sh P2 block (2: full tw16040 extraction set + IsPM). dateutils 57/57 on 5.2, functional on 5.3. Full sweep 1672/0 (kcl 1446 incl. dateutils 57, kklass 226). PLAN CORRECTION: plan's ISO week of 2004-01-01 (W53/2003) was wrong -> W01/2004 (Thursday is always in its own year's week); FPC-ported algo agrees, logged in TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md corrections table. +12 coverage rows.

tasks.title: Simple field extractors
tasks.acceptance: dayOfTheWeek ISO Mon=1..Sun=7 (1970-01-01 -> 4); ISO week fixtures: 2004-01-01 -> W53(2003), 2005-01-01 -> W53(2004), 2008-12-29 -> W1(2009), 2010-01-03 -> W53(2009). FPC parity (tw16040): full extraction set on 2011-03-26 19:15:30.555 (YearOf..MilliSecondOf + HourOfTheDay/MinuteOfTheHour/SecondOfTheMinute/MilliSecondOfTheSecond twins) and IsPM=true.

tasks.title: OfThe* families + week-in-month helpers
tasks.acceptance: Cross-consistency: milliSecondOfTheDay == ((hourOf*60+minuteOf)*60+secondOf)*1000+milliSecondOf; boundary spot checks at year/week starts.

phases.name: Start/End & day predicates
phases.result: 20 functions: startOf/endOf The/A Year/Month/Week/Day (20 incl. twins), isToday, isSameDay, isSameMonth, previousDayOfWeek. startOfADay/endOfADay overload on arg count ((y,m,d) vs (year,dayOfYear)). startOfAWeek/endOfAWeek use the P3-ported internal _encode_date_week (FPC TryEncodeDateWeek; reused by P5). FPC quirks: isSameDay truncates only the basis; previousDayOfWeek takes a weekday NUMBER (1..7) not a date. Verified invariant endOfTheX == startOfNext(X) - 1ms for day/month/year/week. Tests: 007_StartEnd.sh (6), 008_Predicates.sh (5), 002 P3 block (2: StartOfTheYear/EndOfTheYear tw16040). dateutils 70/70 on 5.2, functional on 5.3. Full sweep 1685/0 (kcl 1459 incl. dateutils 70, kklass 226). +11 coverage rows.

tasks.title: startOf*/endOf* + predicates
tasks.acceptance: endOfTheX == startOfNextX - 1ms; week boundaries ISO (Mon..Sun); endOfTheMonth Feb leap fixtures; The/A twins agree on the same inputs. FPC parity (tw16040): StartOfTheYear(2011-03-26)=2011-01-01, EndOfTheYear=2011-12-31 23:59:59.999.

phases.name: Arithmetic, spans, comparison
phases.goal: Inc*, *Between (approx + exact), periodBetween, *Span, withinPast*, compare/same, ranges.
phases.result: 43 functions: incYear/Month/Week/Day/Hour/Minute/Second/MilliSecond (incMonth+incYear day-clamp); yearsBetween..milliSecondsBetween (years/months approx by default + exact flag via periodBetween); periodBetween (FPC borrow logic); dateTimeDiff (signed ms); yearSpan..milliSecondSpan (6dp); withinPast* (8); compareDateTime/Date/Time (-1/0/1); sameDateTime/Date/Time; dateInRange/timeInRange(overnight wrap)/dateTimeInRange. Integer KDT collapses FPC's float DateTimeToNumber/HalfMilliSecond machinery: *Between = |now-then|/unit, compare = sign(a-b). Span overflow (plan risk #3) AVOIDED via whole/remainder split. Internal helpers _period_between, _span_fixed. Tests: 009_Increment.sh (5), 010_BetweenSpan.sh (6), 011_CompareRange.sh (6). dateutils 87/87 on 5.2, functional on 5.3. Full sweep 1702/0 (kcl 1476 incl. dateutils 87, kklass 226). SECOND PLAN CORRECTION: periodBetween(2004-02-29,2005-02-28) is 0y11m28d not the plan's 30d (FPC algorithm traced); logged in TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md corrections table. +17 coverage rows.

tasks.title: Inc* family
tasks.acceptance: incMonth clamps: 2004-01-31 +1m -> 2004-02-29, 2005-01-31 +1m -> 2005-02-28; incYear on Feb 29 -> Feb 28 next non-leap; negative deltas symmetric; time-of-day preserved.

tasks.title: *Between + periodBetween + *Span
tasks.acceptance: FPC parity: approx constants for years/months (365.25/30.4375 days) with abs(diff) truncation; exact flag switches to periodBetween; periodBetween borrow fixtures (2004-02-29 -> 2005-02-28 = 0y 11m 30d per FPC algorithm — port the algorithm and pin actual FPC values); Span outputs 6dp fixed-point.

tasks.title: withinPast*, compare/same, ranges
tasks.acceptance: compare* echo -1/0/1; ranges honor inclusive flag (dateInRange default true per FPC); withinPast == (between <= range).

phases.name: Recode & week/day encodings
phases.goal: Field surgery + ISO week-date and day-of-week-in-month encodings.
phases.result: 19 functions: recodeYear/Month/Day/Hour/Minute/Second/MilliSecond, recodeDate/Time/DateTime, tryRecodeDateTime ('-' = leave field), encodeDateWeek/tryEncodeDateWeek/decodeDateWeek, encodeDateMonthWeek/tryEncodeDateMonthWeek/decodeDateMonthWeek, encodeDayOfWeekInMonth/tryEncodeDayOfWeekInMonth. New internal helpers _recode, _encode_date_month_week, _encode_dow_in_month (reuse P2/P3 _decode_date_week/_encode_date_week/_decode_date_month_week). Tests: 012_Recode.sh (5), 013_WeekDayEncodings.sh (5). dateutils 97/97 on 5.2, functional on 5.3. Full sweep 1712/0 (kcl 1486 incl. dateutils 97, kklass 226). THIRD PLAN NOTE: encodeDayOfWeekInMonth has NO negative-Nth/'last' form in FPC (ANthDayOfWeek is Word); plan's P5.2 'negative Nth' acceptance is not an FPC feature — logged in corrections table. +10 coverage rows.

tasks.title: recode* family
tasks.acceptance: '-' keeps a field; invalid recombination (e.g. recodeMonth of Jan-31 to Feb) fails with status 1 exactly like FPC raises; tryRecodeDateTime status-only.

tasks.title: Week-date and day-of-week encodings
tasks.acceptance: decode(encode) roundtrip on the ISO fixture table incl. W53/W1 boundaries; encodeDayOfWeekInMonth with negative Nth (last Friday) parity with FPC.

phases.name: Conversions & timezones
phases.goal: Unix, ISO 8601 with zones, Julian, local<->UTC, time intervals.
phases.result: 21 functions: dateTimeToUnix/unixToDateTime, dateTimeToJulianDate/julianDateToDateTime/tryJulian, dateTimeToModifiedJulianDate/modifiedJulian/tryModifiedJulian, localTimeToUniversal/universalTimeToLocal, encodeTimeInterval/tryEncodeTimeInterval, dateToISO8601/iso8601ToDate/iso8601ToDateDef/tryISO8601ToDate, tryISOStrToDate/Time/DateTime, isoTZStrToTZOffset/tryISOTZStrToTZOffset. KDT makes JD trivial: JD = 2440587.5 + KDT/86400000 (1970-01-01 = JD 2440587.5), exact integer-numerator math; Unix = KDT/1000. FPC sign conventions matched per-function (isoTZStrToTZOffset negates '+03:00'->-180; universalTimeToLocal uses minutes-east). Internal helpers _jd_str_to_ms, _normalize_offset_min_east. Tests: 014_UnixJulian.sh (8), 015_ISO8601.sh (8), 002 P6 block (5: tw16040 Julian/MJD + tunitdt1 far-future Unix). NOTE: JD 6-dp output has ~86ms resolution (documented); midnight/noon exact, intraday within 87ms. dateutils 117/117 on 5.2, functional on 5.3. Full sweep 1732/0 (kcl 1506 incl. dateutils 117, kklass 226). tw16040 parity now complete except scanDateTime (P7). +15 coverage rows.

tasks.title: Unix + ISO 8601 conversions
tasks.acceptance: unix anchors (0, 86400, negative); ISO with Z/±hh:mm offsets converts per FPC aUTC flag semantics; cross-check 100 random epochs against GNU date -u when available (skipped-not-failed if absent).

tasks.title: Local<->UTC + Julian + time intervals
tasks.acceptance: JD anchors: 2000-01-01 12:00 UTC == JD 2451545.0; MJD == JD - 2400000.5; offset param form ±minutes and ±hh:mm both accepted; interval hours may exceed 24. FPC parity: tw16040 JD 2455277.5<->2010-03-22 and 2455278.5<->2010-03-23 both directions + MJD roundtrips; tunitdt1 far-future Unix (15796372693 -> 2470-07-26 09:18:13; 2345-12-12 04:45:49 roundtrip).

phases.name: scanDateTime, docs, bench, closeout
phases.goal: Practical parser subset, API docs, performance proof, final sweep.
phases.result: scanDateTime practical subset (yyyy/yy/mm/dd/hh/nn/ss/zzz tokens, NN=minute, quoted literals, elastic whitespace, 2-digit-year pivot 50, epoch defaults) — completes the tw16040 parity file (all 17 assertions). Decision gate P7.1: subset sufficient, full FPC matcher (names/ampm/[]) stays out of scope. README.md written (185-method API reference by group + KDT contract + conventions). TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md finalized (~107 non-FPC rows, 3-file spot-audit, 3 plan corrections, final class stats). bench.sh: all hot paths <= 0.3ms/call on 5.2 AND 5.3 (yearOf 0.125, incDay 0.041, incMonth 0.221, weekOfTheYear 0.250ms), zero forks (empty-PATH verified). Tests: 016_ScanDateTime.sh (5), 002 P7 assertion. FINAL: dateutils 123/123 on 5.2, functional on 5.3; full sweep 1738/0 (kcl 1512 incl. dateutils 123, kklass 226). PROJECT COMPLETE.

tasks.title: scanDateTime practical subset (decision gate)
tasks.fix: Patterns: yyyy yy mm dd hh nn ss zzz + quoted/plain literals + whitespace elasticity. Gate: review whether ampm/timezone/[] optionals are actually needed downstream before extending.
tasks.acceptance: Fixture table of pattern/input/expected; malformed -> status 1. FPC parity (tw16040, final assertion): scanDateTime 'YYYY.MM.DD HH:NN:SS:ZZZ' '2011.03.29 16:46:56:777' == encodeDateTime 2011 3 29 16 46 56 777 — completes the parity file.

tasks.title: README.md API reference + finalize TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md
tasks.acceptance: Every public function listed with signature, echo format and one example; KDT contract and conventions section; link from PLAN.md. TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md reviewed for completeness: every non-FPC test case has its row (spot-audit 3 random test files), summary stats (N cases: X boundary / Y bash-convention / Z representation / W cross-check) added for the analysis pass.

tasks.title: Micro-benchmark + final sweep
tasks.acceptance: Bench: yearOf/incDay/daysBetween <= ~0.3 ms/call on bash 5.2 (thin dispatch, zero forks); full kcl+kklass sweep green on 5.2; dateutils suite green on 5.3; thin-dispatch shape pinned by a test (no static vars crept in); ledger statuses closed.
